title: Manager
description:
• Carry out cross-functional PMO activities in APAC region and support projects.
  • Contribute to smooth execution of key projects together with project members.
  • Provide effective support to help project members achieve project goals.
  • Support risk management and QCD control covering both contractual and technical aspects for major projects in APAC across all phases, including proposal, bidding, implementation, and operation to contribute to the achievement of project goals.
  • Identify risks associated with contractual and technical conditions, formulate response strategies, and implement ongoing, monitoring to enhance comprehensive project risk management.
  • Improve project’s QCD and operations, as well as take the lead in implementing those methods within the project.
  • Thoroughly adhere to a hands-on approach and work with the team to solve problems collaboratively.
  • Responsible for the growth of business for the APAC region and achieving all organizational goals, such as budget, timelines, quality, and deliverables.
  • Sharing product issues, delivery challenges, knowledge, and know-how gained through the PMO across CA and HQ, and promote global collaboration.
  • Communicate well with headquarters in Japan to understand its strategy, policy, and cutting-edge solutions and share with ASEAN team members.
  • Build up and keep relationships with APAC members.
  • Strengthen, lead and motivate the talent in your Team.


Requirement:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or Engineering, or Business related field.
  • More than 10 years of working experience in public safety business as a project manager of medium to large projects
  • More than 5 years of working experience with governmental organization in public safety business
  • PM related certificates (such as PMP, ITIL)
  • Project management experience in system delivery.
  • Risk management experience.
  • Hands-on knowledge of QCD control
  • Experience in coaching and guiding other new project managers
  • Development of methodologies such as Waterfall and Agile.
  • Experience in working with a diverse, geographically remote team.
  • Strong leadership skills to lead and motivate the talents in team
  • Effective influencing, organizational and problem-solving skills
  • Excellent interpersonal, negotiation, communication skills and ability to interact with all levels.
  • Strong presentation skills and ability to write professionally in both English and Japanese.
  • Proficiency in the Japanese language (JLPT N1) to liaise with NEC’s headquarters in Japan.
